text: This space radiates the sterility and structure of a modern institution, almost clinical in its composure. As I stand outside the glass meeting room, observing the neat arrangement of chairs and the hushed glow of screens, I sense anticipation and a muted tension. Stepping into the R+D exhibit, I read words about confusion, longing, and the awkwardness of being perceived or misunderstood. These reflections echo within me; the environment’s technological sharpness juxtaposes with the fragile humanity of the displayed thoughts. I feel both curiosity and a gentle unease, reminded of the delicate balance between being seen and remaining unseen in spaces like these.
